rag_rules: Place AGREE assumptions and guarantees in component type scopes for port-level contracts, and in implementation scopes for subcomponent bindings.
Use visible identifiers from the AADL model for all AGREE expressions and declarations.
generic_rules: AGREE annex blocks must be syntactically valid and scoped to their owning component.
Do not redeclare AADL ports as AGREE variables; they are already visible in scope.
Use only valid AADL feature names and types in AGREE contracts.
Assignments in AGREE should refer to valid implementation-level outputs or subcomponent bindings.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assumptions and guarantees must be labeled with quoted strings and follow valid expression syntax.
Constants and equations in AGREE use `const name : type = value;` and `eq name : type = expression;`.
anti_patterns: Do not reference subcomponent ports or implementation-only paths in component type contracts.
Do not invent or redeclare AADL feature names in AGREE; use only visible identifiers.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations as AGREE variables
